source: PPAC / Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas — approximate, dated reference (no clean free live feed exists)

crude.note: The Indian Basket is the crude mix India actually buys (sour Oman/Dubai + Brent). PPAC prices it daily; this is a recent reference level.

lpg.note: 14.2 kg domestic refill, metro reference price. PMUY (Ujjwala) beneficiaries get a per-cylinder subsidy.

priceMechanism: Since 2017 OMCs revise petrol/diesel daily in principle, but in practice pump prices move far less than crude — they tend to rise quickly when crude climbs and fall slowly when it drops, with tax absorbing much of the swing.
lastPriceRevisionNote: Last nationwide pump-price revision: a ₹2/litre cut on 14 Mar 2024. Prices had been frozen since May 2022 before that, and have barely moved since — even as crude swung.

ethanol.targetNote: 20% average ethanol blending in petrol (E20) — reached in 2025, about five years ahead of the 2030 target.
ethanol.mileageNote: E20 can lower fuel economy by roughly 1–6% in engines not tuned for it; newer E20-ready vehicles see less.
ethanol.why: Ethanol is blended into petrol mainly to cut crude imports and support cane/grain farmers.

taxRevenue.note: Approximate annual government revenue from petroleum taxes — central excise plus state VAT. One of the largest single revenue lines for both the Centre and the states.

importSources.note: Before the 2022 Ukraine war Russia was barely 2% of India's crude; discounted Russian barrels then became India's single largest source. Shares shift month to month — this is a recent reference mix, not a live feed.
importSources.why: Buying discounted Russian crude cut India's import bill by billions of dollars a year and reshaped who India buys oil from — the biggest change in the country's oil map in decades.

refining.note: India refines far more than it consumes and exports the surplus as diesel, petrol and jet fuel — refined petroleum products are among the country's largest single export earners. Reliance's Jamnagar complex is the world's largest single-site refinery.
refining.why: India imports crude but exports fuel: it buys cheap crude, refines it at scale, and sells the finished product abroad at a margin. That refining muscle is why the import bill and the export ledger both run on oil.

strategicReserve.note: India's dedicated Strategic Petroleum Reserve holds about 5.33 million tonnes of crude — roughly 9–10 days of imports — in underground caverns. Counting the crude and product that refiners and OMCs hold commercially, total cover is around 74 days. India is expanding Phase-II caverns to deepen this buffer.
strategicReserve.why: With 88% of crude imported, days of cover is national-security arithmetic: it is how long the country could keep refineries running if imports were suddenly cut off.
